summary: Full free-form sweep + tutorial walkthrough on dev-b4c40c5 against http://localhost:8080. Prior regressions (`run capture --lines` negative bound, `clier version`/`team list`/`run list` swallowing stray positional args, `run stop` idempotency) all stay fixed. Two new fails: (1) `team update --patch-json '{"protocol":null}'` returns a server error that *appends a recovery hint* — `(use "" for empty value, or :reset-protocol for default protocol)` — which contradicts Trust 3's no-coaching contract, and the `:reset-protocol` token in that hint is also a phantom syntax (the actual command is `clier team reset-protocol <ns/name>`, no colon, separate verb); (2) `clier run tell` silently picks the positional arg and discards piped stdin when both are supplied, instead of refusing the ambiguity loudly — help text presents the two as an `or`. Everything else exercised cleanly: error envelopes are loud and named, multi-field violations surface together, JSON shape stays stable with RFC3339 timestamps and `[]` for empty arrays, mutations are queryable through `team get` / `run view`, idempotency holds for star/unstar/run-stop/auth-login, subteam cycles are caught at `run start`, and disposable resources cleaned up with no orphans.

findings.title: clier --help enumerates the full subcommand surface
findings.rationale: Trust 1 — `clier --help` lists every top-level command (auth, open, run, team, tutorial, version) and each subcommand's --help recurses cleanly into the next layer (auth login/logout/status; open dashboard; run attach/capture/list/start/stop/tell/view; team create/delete/get/list/reset-protocol/star/unstar/update). Nothing was reachable that wasn't documented, and nothing in the help text was a dead reference.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
Available Commands:
  auth           Log in and manage credentials
  open           Open clier surfaces in your browser
  run            Start, message, and stop tmux runs
  team           Browse and manage teams on the server
  tutorial       Walk through your first multi-agent run
  version        Print the build identity (binary, channel, server URL)

findings.title: First-run pointer to clier tutorial is present in top-level help
findings.rationale: Trust 1 — `clier --help` opens with `First run:  clier tutorial` so a fresh agent can orient with zero prior knowledge.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
First run:  clier tutorial
Per command: clier <command> --help

findings.title: clier version returns build identity JSON with stable keys
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — `clier version` returns one JSON object with channel/commit/dashboard_url/server_url/version. All keys present, no nulls.

findings.title: Tutorial step 1 (clier team list) matches behaviour
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — running the literal command from the tutorial returned the catalog including jakeraft/hello-clier as the demo team, exactly as the tutorial promises.

findings.title: Tutorial step 2 (clier run start jakeraft/hello-clier) mints a run
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — `run start` printed run_id, run_dir, session_name, namespace, team_name, and one entry per agent (hello-clier + hello-codex subteam) — exactly the shape the tutorial says to note for the next steps.

findings.title: Tutorial step 3 (heredoc tell) accepted
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — the heredoc form shown in the tutorial (`clier run tell --run <id> --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF' ... EOF`) was accepted and persisted; `run view` later showed the message in messages[] with from=null, to=jakeraft.hello-clier, content=verbatim, created_at=RFC3339.
findings.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-091231-50626f5d --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF' Greet your peer ... EOF

findings.title: Tutorial step 4 (clier run capture <run-id>) returns pane snapshots
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — `run capture` returned data[] with one entry per agent: agent_id / captured_at (RFC3339 with offset) / content / window. Tutorial promises 'JSON, no tmux attach required' and that's exactly the shape.

findings.title: Tutorial step 5 (clier run attach) skipped — needs interactive TTY
findings.skip_reason: Step 5 hands the terminal to tmux and requires an interactive TTY; the QA harness has no TTY. The tutorial itself flags this as the interactive step.
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — non-TTY behaviour cannot be exercised here; documented as a skip per the skill rule on interactive steps.

references.label: step 5
references.excerpt:
5. clier run attach <run-id>
   When you do want to watch live, attach. Detach with Ctrl-b d.

findings.title: Tutorial step 6 (clier run stop <run-id>) tears down cleanly
findings.rationale: Trust 2 + Trust 6 — `run stop` returned {"stopped":true} exit 0, the run dir under ~/.clier/runs/<run-id>/ was removed, and `run list` no longer surfaces the run. Exactly what the tutorial promises ('tmux session, clones, run.json — all gone').

findings.title: team create → get → update → delete pipeline succeeds
findings.rationale: Trust 2 + Trust 4 — full team lifecycle exercised against the disposable prefix. Each mutation has a queryable read-side via `team get` and `team list`.

steps.label: create
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507090925-claude --agent-type claude --command claude --git-repo-url https://github.com/jakeraft/hello-clier --description 'QA disposable team'

findings.title: team star / unstar are idempotent (help promises this)
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — `team star` and `team unstar` help advertises idempotence ('Idempotent: starring an already-starred team is a no-op'). Verified: a second call returns the same JSON shape and exit 0 in both directions.

steps.label: unstar an already-unstarred team is also a no-op (idempotent in both directions)

findings.title: team delete clears the row from get and list
findings.rationale: Trust 6 — after `team delete`, `team get` returns the standard 404 envelope and `team list --namespace jakeraft` no longer surfaces the team. A second `team delete` on the same key returns 404 (delete does not promise idempotence in --help, so this is by design — contrast with `run stop` which does promise it).

steps.label: get after delete = 404
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/qa-20260507090925-claude not found

steps.label: list does not surface deleted
steps.command: clier team list --namespace jakeraft | grep -c qa-20260507090925-claude

steps.label: second delete = 404 (no idempotence claim in --help)
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/qa-20260507090925-claude not found

findings.title: Cannot delete a team referenced as a subteam
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `team delete --help` says 'A team that is referenced as a subteam by another team cannot be deleted directly — the referencing team must drop the link first'. Verified: created codex with --subteam claude, then `team delete claude` returned 409 with the exact precondition.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Failed precondition: team is referenced as a subteam by another team

findings.title: team cannot be its own subteam (self-loop is rejected)
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `team update --subteam <self>` is refused with a named 422 Invalid argument; the offender path `subteams[0]` identifies which entry failed.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: subteams[0]: team cannot be its own subteam

findings.title: Subteam cycles are caught at run start (not at update time)
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — server allows building a 2-node bidirectional subteam graph (claude→codex AND codex→claude) via two separate `team update` calls; cycle detection is enforced at `run start` instead, with a loud, named 409: `subteam cycle detected: jakeraft/qa-20260507090925-claude`. This is acceptable because every individual `team get` exposes its direct subteams transparently, so the cycle is reachable by graph-walk and cannot ship to a tmux pane.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Failed precondition: subteam cycle detected: jakeraft/qa-20260507090925-claude

findings.title: team update rejects immutable fields via --patch-json
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `team update --help` says 'Immutable fields (namespace, name, agent_type) cannot be patched'. Verified all three are rejected with a single named 422.

findings.rationale: Trust 3 — bare `team update <key>` is refused with a single self-contained line listing the available flags.

findings.title: team update --patch-json refuses malformed JSON loudly
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — invalid JSON body fails client-side with a single error line, exit 1.

findings.title: team update --subteam '' clears the subteam list to []
findings.rationale: Trust 4/5 — passing an empty string per `--help` clears subteams; the response object exposes `"subteams": []` (not null, not absent).

findings.title: team create surfaces every offender in one response and enforces ownership
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — when multiple fields fail validation, the server returns ALL of them in one error line (no short-circuit); a 409 fires on duplicates and a 403 fires when the namespace doesn't match the caller's login.

findings.title: team list pagination + filters + sort enforce bounds and emit valid cursors
findings.rationale: Trust 3 + Trust 5 — `--page-size`, `--sort`, `--agent-type`, `--page-token`, and `--q` all enforce documented bounds with named 422s; valid pagination returns an opaque base64 `next_cursor` that resolves on a follow-up call; an empty result set still returns `data: []` and `meta: {has_next:false, next_cursor:""}` (collections never null).

findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `run tell`, `run view`, `run capture`, and `run start` all refuse loudly: missing flags via Cobra's required-flag line, unknown run via `run not found in ~/.clier/runs`, unknown agent (incl. unknown --from sender) via `agent not found in run: <id>`, empty content via `message content is empty`, unknown team via 404, malformed team key via client-side single line.

findings.title: run stop is idempotent on already-stopped and unknown ids
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — `run stop --help` says 'Stop is final and idempotent'. Verified: a second stop on the same id and a stop on an unknown id both return `{"stopped":true}` exit 0. (Prior report qa-20260507-073601 flagged this as failing on dev-01e85e9 — fixed since.)

findings.rationale: Trust 6 — after `run stop` on 20260507-091448-81c047d9 and 20260507-091231-50626f5d, only the active QA run dir 20260507-090503-deb91832 remains under ~/.clier/runs/, `run list` returns the remaining live runs only, and `run view <stopped>` returns the standard not-found error.

findings.title: run view emits the same key set on repeated calls and starts with messages: []
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — two consecutive `run view` calls return identical key sets (agents, messages, namespace, run_dir, run_id, session_name, started_at, status, stopped_at, team_name). A freshly-started run shows messages: [] (not null, not absent), stopped_at: null (scalar nullable timestamp), and started_at as RFC3339 with offset.

findings.title: run tell mutation is queryable via run view messages[]
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — every `run tell` lands as one entry in `run view`'s messages[] with from / to / content / created_at (RFC3339). When --from is omitted, from=null (nullable scalar — acceptable per skill: 'Empty collections as []' applies to arrays, not scalars).
findings.stdout_excerpt: {"messages":[{"from":null,"to":"jakeraft.hello-clier","content":"Greet your peer and tell me what you learned.","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:12:41.996121+09:00"},{"from":null,"to":"jakeraft.hello-codex",...},{"from":null,"to":"jakeraft.hello-clier","content":"arg content","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:14:24.92614+09:00"}]}

findings.title: All error paths print one stderr line starting with `error: ` and exit non-zero
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — server validation (4xx), client-side validation, Cobra's unknown-command/flag handler, and stray positional args all emit one self-contained stderr line beginning with `error: ` and exit 1. Prior regression (`clier version <stray>` / `team list <stray>` / `run list <stray>` swallowed extras silently) stays fixed on dev-b4c40c5.

findings.title: Every timestamp surfaces as RFC3339 with offset
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — sampled timestamps across `team get` (created_at/updated_at), `run start` and `run view` (started_at), `run capture` (captured_at), `run view` (messages[].created_at) — every one is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.ffffff+09:00`, no Z-only / no naive forms.

findings.title: auth login is a no-op when a session already exists
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — `auth login --help` says 'If a valid session already exists this command is a no-op — it prints the current login as JSON and exits 0'. Verified: on the pre-authenticated session, `auth login` exits 0 with `{"login":"jakeraft"}` on stdout, and the human-readable `note: already logged in as jakeraft; run 'clier auth logout' first to switch accounts` routes to stderr (not stdout — JSON parse stays clean).

steps.label: stderr carries the human note
steps.stderr_excerpt: note: already logged in as jakeraft; run 'clier auth logout' first to switch accounts

findings.title: team update --patch-json '{"protocol":null}' error appends a recovery hint with a phantom ':reset-protocol' token
findings.fail_note: Two problems in one error line. (1) Trust 3 says 'The CLI does not append recovery hints; the agent rediscovers its next move from clier --help' — but this error appends a parenthetical hint: `(use "" for empty value, or :reset-protocol for default protocol)`. The error should stop at `protocol: must not be null` (or equivalent), then let the agent re-orient via --help. (2) The recovery hint references `:reset-protocol` with a leading colon — a token that does not exist in the CLI surface. The actual command is `clier team reset-protocol <ns/name>` (separate verb, no colon, full key required); `team update --patch-json '{":reset-protocol":true}'` is itself rejected as `:reset-protocol: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)`. So the hint is both unwarranted and misleading. The team update --help text correctly references the verb form ('Restoring protocol to the default is a separate action — \'team reset-protocol\' (PATCH cannot express it)') — the inconsistency is between the help text and the runtime error.
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — error messages must name *what* failed and stop; appending coaching is exactly the contract this trust point exists to prevent, and embedding a phantom token (`:reset-protocol`) inside the coaching makes the violation worse, not better.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: protocol: must not be null (use "" for empty value, or :reset-protocol for default protocol)

references.label: team update --help (correct verb form)
references.excerpt: Restoring protocol to the default is a separate action — 'team reset-protocol' (PATCH cannot express it).

references.label: Trust 3 contract
references.excerpt: The CLI does not append recovery hints; the agent rediscovers its next move from `clier --help`.

findings.title: run tell silently picks the positional arg and discards piped stdin when both are supplied
findings.fail_note: Help text describes the two as alternatives ('Content can be passed as a positional arg (single line) or piped on stdin (multi-line)') — the word 'or' implies exclusivity. In practice, when both are supplied, the implementation silently picks the positional and discards the stdin payload with no warning. Verified by piping `stdin content` and passing `arg content` as the positional: `run view` later showed `"content":"arg content"` in messages[] — stdin was dropped on the floor. Trust 3 says ambiguous input should be loud and named; the contract should either be (a) reject when both are present with a single named error, or (b) document the precedence rule explicitly in --help. Today neither holds.
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — silent precedence rules are exactly the surface the agent cannot recover from via --help. The fact that the discarded payload is the one with multi-line capability makes the silent behaviour particularly easy to miss.
findings.command: echo 'stdin content' | clier run tell --run 20260507-091231-50626f5d --to jakeraft.hello-clier 'arg content'

references.label: run tell --help
references.excerpt: Content can be passed as a positional arg (single line) or piped on stdin (multi-line).

references.label: verified delivered content via run view
